title: Bloom Energy Capacity Expansion to 2 GW by December 2026
text: Bloom Energy announced plans to double its annual production capacity from approximately 1 GW to 2 GW by December 2026. The company currently operates about 1.4 GW of deployed energy systems across more than 1,000 locations in nine countries. Q3 2025 financial performance showed revenue of $519M, up 57.1% year-over-year, with operating income of $7.8M, swinging to profit from a $9.7M loss in Q3 2024. CEO KR Sridhar described the AI data center opportunity as a once-in-a-generation opportunity to redefine how power is generated for commercial and industrial sectors. Bloom's fuel cell technology achieves 15%-20% better fuel efficiency compared to combustion turbines. The company announced a $5 billion strategic partnership with Brookfield Asset Management for on-site power solutions, with Brookfield reportedly investing $50 billion in AI infrastructure and tripling its AI strategy over three years. The company now competes in power-hungry markets across the Midwest, Mid-Atlantic, and Texas, beyond traditional high-cost electricity regions like California and the Northeast.
